current_fact.fact_text: In 1975, India launched Aryabhata, its very first satellite, from the Kapustin Yar cosmodrome in Russia. Named after an ancient Indian mathematician-astronomer, it marked India’s bold entry into the space age with help from another spacefaring nation.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

current_fact.match_reason: A first national voyage into orbit fits with a ‘Destination’ episode about collective journeys into new territories.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1818, Augustin-Jean Fresnel submitted his "Note on the Theory of Diffraction", ending with the mathematical expressions now known as the Fresnel integrals. His wave-based view of light helped overturn older ideas and explained why light can bend around obstacles.

episode.title: Morning Drops #52- Think Again
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the quiet art of changing your mind, guided by Adam Grant's book Think Again. From the beliefs we carry without questioning to the identities we build around what we think we know, this episode follows the slow, sometimes uncomfortable process of unlearning.

Along the way, we meet a Serbian trumpet player who had to let go of one dream to find another, a Tunisian oud master who challenged centuries of tradition from the inside, and a Japanese band who found freedom in playing for no one. The music moves from Balkan brass to Mediterranean stillness to psychedelic drift, tracing the shape of a mind opening up.

Listen slowly, and ask yourself: is there something you believe today that deserves a second look?

Tracklist:
Boban Marković Orkestar – SAT
Anouar Brahem – The Astounding Eyes of Rita
Kikagaku Moyo – Dripping Sun
Son Lux – Lost It to Trying

queue.match_reason: Fresnel’s radical rethinking of light embodies the ‘Think Again’ spirit of questioning old certainties with new perspectives.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1984, "Advance Australia Fair" was officially proclaimed Australia’s national anthem, and green and gold were confirmed as the country’s national colours. The song replaced "God Save the Queen", reflecting a growing desire for a distinct Australian identity.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Soul Sisters
episode.description: A tribute to four iconic voices — women who have shaped the way we listen, feel, and move. Each track carries a different kind of presence: one that holds you, one that questions, one that grounds, and one that lets go. An energy that is soulful, raw, and unapologetically feminine.
songs: Grace Jones - Walking in the Rain
Aretha Franklin - A Rose Is Still a Rose

queue.match_reason: An anthem and colours that define a nation’s voice resonate with an episode celebrating powerful voices that shape cultural identity.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1770, Lieutenant James Cook and his crew first sighted the eastern coast of what is now Australia. To European maps, this line of shore was a blank space being filled in, though Indigenous peoples had known and named it for tens of thousands of years.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: Cook’s long sea voyage and altered worldview harmonize with an episode about how travel quietly reshapes perception.

queue.fact_text: In 1713, Holy Roman Emperor Charles VI issued the Pragmatic Sanction of 1713 to allow his future daughters to inherit the Habsburg lands. It was a preemptive legal maneuver to keep a sprawling empire intact, years before his famous daughter Maria Theresa was even born.

episode.title: Morning Drops #46 - Fog and Fire
episode.description: In today’s Morning Drops, Gaia brings us on a passport-free trip between two Italian energies: the foggy stillness of the North and the volcanic pulse of the South. Throughout the journey, Gaia shares four of her favourite Italian songwriters. Grab your coffee and tune into the radio show from another planet!
songs: Edoardo Bennato - La Torre Di Babele
Lucio Dalla - Com'è Profondo il Mare

queue.match_reason: A high-stakes dynastic compromise between cold calculation and passionate power matches an episode about navigating contrasting energies and regions.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 65 AD, a freedman named Milichus betrayed the Pisonian conspiracy to assassinate Emperor Nero. His decision exposed a wide-ranging plot among Rome’s elite and triggered a brutal wave of executions.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A conspiracy, betrayal, and abuse of power in imperial Rome parallel an episode centered on music that confronts systems of oppression.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 2008, a fire broke out in the Ultratumba nightclub in Quito, Ecuador, killing 19 people and injuring many more. The tragedy drew attention to unsafe venues and lax enforcement of fire regulations in crowded urban nightlife spaces.

episode.title: Morning Drops #51 - The Dancefloor
episode.description:
For International Women’s Day, this Morning Drop turns its focus to women on the dancefloor, not as a trend, but as a cultural force.

Across five tracks, Gaia follows a line from disco closers to hip hop club classics, tracing how the dancefloor became a place where women could take space, build confidence, and create connection, sometimes with friends, sometimes alone, always with music at the centre.

This episode isn’t about pointing fingers. It’s a gentle reminder that spaces like the dancefloor still matter: places where people can express themselves, meet each other with respect, and feel part of something bigger than a night out.

Tracklist:
Donna Summer - Last Dance (1978)
The Sequence  - Funk you up
Salt-N-Pepa - Push It
Queen Latifah - Ladies First
Alicia Bridges - I Love the Nightlife

queue.match_reason: A nightclub disaster underscores how fragile dancefloors can be, pairing with an episode that treats the dancefloor as a meaningful, protected space.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1971, Sierra Leone officially became a republic, ending its status as a constitutional monarchy, with Siaka Stevens as its first president. The change was as much symbolic as political, marking a new phase of self-definition after independence.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: A West African nation redefining its political identity fits with an Afrobeat-focused episode celebrating African cultural and musical self-expression.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1942, during World War II, the Majdan-Tatarski ghetto was established in German-occupied Poland between the Lublin Ghetto and a subcamp of Majdanek. It became another node in the network of forced segregation and extermination targeting Jewish communities.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: A somber historical moment of confinement and suffering suits a reflective, slow-paced episode about vulnerability, pain, and gradual healing.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1927, actress and writer Mae West was sentenced to ten days in jail for "obscenity" because of her play "Sex". The publicity turned her into a sensation and highlighted how women who spoke openly about desire could scandalize – and reshape – popular culture.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

queue.match_reason: Mae West’s defiant, taboo-breaking voice in the 1920s mirrors an episode celebrating fearless women in rap challenging norms around gender and sexuality.

queue.fact_text: In 1925, on this day, the Chilean football club Colo-Colo was founded in Santiago by David Arellano and fellow players who split from another team. Over time, Colo-Colo would become the most successful and widely supported football club in Chile.

episode.title: Sedna FM #05 - On The Go - Zero Zero
episode.description: An energetic mix for when you're on the move. Zero Zero captures that feeling of starting fresh, moving forward, and embracing the journey. Perfect for travel, walks, or just getting things done.
songs: Grace Jones - Pull up to the bumper
Easy star all star - Soul love
James Brown - Talking loud and saying nothing (remix)

queue.match_reason: The founding of a passionate, movement-filled football club aligns with a high-energy, on-the-move mix perfect for crowds, chants, and stadium spirit.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1976, a violent F5 tornado struck near Brownwood, Texas, injuring 11 people. Astonishingly, two people were reportedly thrown about 1,000 yards (around 910 meters) by the storm and survived without injury.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: The raw, chaotic force of an F5 tornado pairs with an ‘electric shock’ rock episode full of high-voltage guitar storms.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1956, Hollywood star Grace Kelly married Prince Rainier III of Monaco, leaving her film career to become a real-life princess. The wedding fused cinema glamour with European royalty, captivating audiences around the world.

episode.title: Sedna FM #04 - Queen of Spades
episode.description: Tuning in from the planet of irrational love. Exploring love's most vulnerable side — the kind that awakens the child within us. A diverse music selection ranging from electric blues to downtempo, soul, and jazz. A tribute to the feeling that has moved artists to create the most beautiful works. Love makes us vulnerable, and there is no art without vulnerability.
songs: Alabama Shakes - Gimme All Your Love
Queens of the stone age - Make it wit chu
Etta James - I'd Rather Go Blind
Eric Clapton - Have you ever loved a woman

queue.match_reason: A royal romance that blends fantasy and sacrifice aligns with an episode centered on the vulnerability and drama of love.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1810, the people of Caracas forced Governor Vicente Emparán to resign and installed a local junta, marking Venezuela’s first step toward home rule. What started as a confrontation on a balcony helped ignite a broader independence movement in South America.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: A Latin American fight for political self-determination connects naturally to Residente’s politically charged, Latin-rooted musical resistance.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1782, John Adams secured Dutch recognition of the United States as an independent nation. The house he purchased in The Hague to act as his base later became the first American embassy in the world.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

queue.match_reason: Adams’ diplomatic mission across the Atlantic and his embassy ‘harbor’ pair with a sea-bound episode about international companionship and shared journeys.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1809, during the Napoleonic Wars’ War of the Fifth Coalition, an Austrian corps was defeated by the Duchy of Warsaw at Raszyn, while Austria’s main army lost to Marshal Davout at Teugen-Hausen. These sharp blows helped secure a French advantage in a fast-moving campaign.

episode.title: Morning Drops #20 - Hitchhiking Poland
episode.description: Yasmin takes you back to the summer of 2020, when borders were still uncertain and a long-held wish to visit Poland finally came true. Through four tracks, tracing her journey from a stranger's couch in Poznań to the peaks of Zakopane.

queue.match_reason: A pivotal Napoleonic battle on Polish soil connects with an episode built around personal travel stories and music from contemporary Poland.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1987, The Simpsons made their first appearance as a series of animated shorts on The Tracey Ullman Show, beginning with "Good Night". These rough, early sketches of a yellow cartoon family would grow into one of the longest-running TV series in history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - secret_mp3_player_1
episode.description: Inspired by a throwback MP3 player on Instagram, Yasmin and Gaia take you on a nostalgic journey to the early 2000s. LimeWire, eMule, guilty-pleasure playlists — remember those? Hit play and relive the songs that shaped a generation.

queue.match_reason: The Simpsons’ scrappy TV debut is a perfect match for an episode steeped in pop nostalgia and formative screen-and-sound memories.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 2001, while Endeavour carried Canadarm2 skyward, another kind of arm—this time musical—was already embracing listeners worldwide: hip hop, R&B, and global grooves were tying cultures together much like the space station ties modules in orbit. This date marks a moment where both space engineering and music were quietly building new kinds of connection.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: As Canadarm2 begins Sedna-like work linking modules in orbit, Sedna FM’s first episode weaves worldwide genres into one connective ‘life soundtrack’ launched into the universe.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1971, while Salyut 1 was heading for orbit, back on Earth artists and listeners were reimagining how to stretch time through sound—creating long, atmospheric pieces that felt almost weightless. These experiments mirrored the new experience of living and working in microgravity, where each orbit turns minutes into something dreamlike.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: A ‘slow planet’ of orbiting metal and drifting crew members resonates with downtempo, weightless sounds designed for breathing room and orbital calm.

queue.fact_text: On April 19, 1984, as Australia chose "Advance Australia Fair" as its anthem, it also officially embraced green and gold—colours drawn from its unique flora. Wattle blossoms, eucalypts, and endless sunlit fields helped define an identity through the palette of the land itself.

episode.title: Morning Drops #21 - Endless Summer
episode.description: Recorded from Fano, a quiet town on the Adriatic coast where summer stretches long and slow. Between swims, simple meals, and time with family, Gaia reflects on the kind of music that helps us return to ourselves. Through different shades of jazz — from minimal and meditative to warm and rhythmic — an invitation to slip into that endless summer feeling.

queue.match_reason: Sun-drenched national colours rooted in landscape pair with an ‘Endless Summer’ episode steeped in warm, glowing, outdoorsy moods.

queue.fact_text: On this day, from Nero’s Rome to Mare Nostrum and beyond, empires have risen and receded like tides—yet our species keeps looking upward and outward. The pattern of April 19 across centuries—plots, proclamations, launches, and first flights—draws a quiet arc from palace intrigue to planetary exploration.

episode.title: Morning Drops #48 - Cosmic Monday
episode.description:
Cosmic Monday is a Morning Drops episode built around four Italian dance records, tracing how disco rhythms slowly became electronic language.
Gaia moves through four tracks like chapters: groove first, then machines, then that bright, futuristic feeling you only get when the bassline is doing the talking. It's not nostalgia, exactly. It's more like returning to a sound that still works — especially on mornings when you need a little extra energy, but you don't want anything aggressive.
Take it as a slow transmission. Let it carry you into the day.
songs: Giovanotti Mondani Meccanici - Don't Ask Me Why

queue.match_reason: A closing, zoomed-out reflection on centuries of April 19 events ending in spaceflight suits a ‘cosmic’ episode that turns dancefloor grooves into futuristic, orbital language.

published.fact_text: On April 19, 2001, Space Shuttle Endeavour soared into orbit on mission STS-100, delivering Canadarm2 to the International Space Station. This robotic arm became one of the ISS’s most important tools, essentially giving the station a ‘mechanical hand’ to build and maintain itself in space.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A literal shift in humanity’s presence in space pairs with an episode about reimagining ‘space’ as possibility and momentum.

published.fact_text: On this day in 1971, the Soviet Union launched Salyut 1, the first-ever space station. For the first time, humans had a semi-permanent home in orbit, turning space from a place you merely visited into somewhere you could live and work.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

published.match_reason: The first ‘home’ in orbit mirrors the launch of a new Sedna format: both open fresh, rule-free spaces to drift and explore.

published.fact_text: On April 19, 2021, NASA’s Ingenuity helicopter became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on another planet. Hovering above the Martian surface for just 39 seconds, it proved that even in the thin air of Mars, humanity could teach a machine to fly.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

published.match_reason: Ingenuity’s weightless hover above Mars echoes the episode’s theme of floating suspension and immersing in unfamiliar environments.

published.fact_text: In 1975, India launched Aryabhata, its very first satellite, from the Kapustin Yar cosmodrome in Russia. Named after an ancient Indian mathematician-astronomer, it marked India’s bold entry into the space age with help from another spacefaring nation.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

published.match_reason: A first national voyage into orbit fits with a ‘Destination’ episode about collective journeys into new territories.
